name: Paracetamol
description: Reduces fever and relieves mild to moderate pain. First-line treatment for headaches, muscle aches, and cold symptoms.
sideEffects: Rare at therapeutic doses. Liver damage at high doses.
contraindications: Severe liver impairment, active liver disease.

name: Ibuprofen
description: Anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic. Used for pain, inflammation, and fever reduction.
sideEffects: GI upset, ulcers, kidney effects with prolonged use.
contraindications: Active GI bleeding, severe kidney/liver disease, third trimester of pregnancy.

name: Amoxicillin
description: Broad-spectrum penicillin antibiotic for bacterial infections including respiratory, urinary, and skin infections.
dosage: 250-500 mg every 8 hours, or 500-875 mg every 12 hours.
sideEffects: Diarrhea, nausea, rash. Allergic reactions possible.
contraindications: Penicillin allergy, infectious mononucleosis (risk of rash).

name: Omeprazole
description: Reduces stomach acid production. Used for GERD, ulcers, and Zollinger-Ellison syndrome.
dosage: 20-40 mg once daily, 30 min before breakfast.
sideEffects: Headache, nausea, diarrhea. Long-term: B12 deficiency, fracture risk.

name: Loratadine
description: Non-drowsy antihistamine for allergic rhinitis and chronic urticaria.

name: Metformin
description: First-line oral medication for type 2 diabetes. Lowers blood glucose by reducing hepatic glucose production.
dosage: 500 mg twice daily, titrate to max 2000 mg/day.
sideEffects: GI upset, metallic taste, rare: lactic acidosis.
contraindications: eGFR <30, acute or chronic metabolic acidosis, severe hepatic impairment.

name: Amlodipine
description: Treats hypertension and angina by relaxing blood vessels.
contraindications: Severe aortic stenosis, unstable angina (not yet on beta-blocker).

name: Cetirizine
description: Second-generation antihistamine for allergies and hives.

name: Azithromycin
description: Macrolide antibiotic for respiratory, skin, and sexually transmitted infections.
dosage: 500 mg day 1, then 250 mg days 2-5. Or 500 mg daily for 3 days.
sideEffects: Nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain. Rare: QT prolongation.
contraindications: History of cholestatic jaundice with azithromycin, myasthenia gravis.

name: Salbutamol
description: Rapid-acting bronchodilator for asthma and COPD symptom relief.
dosage: 1-2 puffs (100-200 mcg) every 4-6 hours as needed.
contraindications: Hypersensitivity. Use with caution in thyrotoxicosis.

name: Atorvastatin
description: Lowers LDL cholesterol and reduces cardiovascular risk.
sideEffects: Myalgia, elevated liver enzymes, rare: rhabdomyolysis.

name: Diclofenac
description: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic for musculoskeletal pain, arthritis, and post-surgical pain.
sideEffects: GI ulceration, headache, dizziness, elevated liver enzymes.
contraindications: Active GI bleeding, severe heart failure, late pregnancy.

name: Prednisolone
description: Synthetic glucocorticoid for inflammation, autoimmune conditions, and allergic reactions.
dosage: Varies widely: 5-60 mg/day depending on condition.
sideEffects: Weight gain, hyperglycemia, osteoporosis, mood changes with long-term use.
contraindications: Systemic fungal infections, live vaccines during immunosuppressive doses.

name: Losartan
description: Treats hypertension and protects kidneys in diabetic nephropathy.
sideEffects: Dizziness, hyperkalemia, cough (less than ACE inhibitors).

name: Pantoprazole
description: Acid-suppressing medication for GERD, peptic ulcers, and H. pylori eradication.
